query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "geneb: why genomic models are hard to compare" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (b) and (e) by measuring the performance of large language models on the specific empirical domain (clinical and biomedical tasks) referenced in the user's question, providing the necessary baseline evidence for how genomic/biomedical models behave in comparison to general LLMs. Although it does not explicitly address the "comparability" framework of the GENEB paper, it establishes the essential empirical landscape of LLM capabilities in the target domain that a lit
